mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.02 during periods of rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate spikes above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations upward and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows, or forward quarterly returns are not significantly positive when both series expand.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing output and supply-side bottlenecks, which compresses profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 adjustments over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive or flat during industrial production contractions, with a t-statistic failing to reject the null hypothesis of zero alpha.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading that jumps above 0.3 percent month-over-month triggers immediate expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in over 60% of months where CPI crosses the threshold, invalidating the tightening fear mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces a negative information coefficient over 3-year rolling periods, or quarterly forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect increased construction activity and durable goods demand, which directly improves earnings forecasts for cyclical sectors and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no positive correlation with housing starts, with a Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in industrial production and a rise in CPI indicates stagflationary supply shocks, which erode real corporate profits and trigger immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than half of the observed stagflationary episodes, or the interaction term fails to achieve statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.
